body:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="UTF-8" standalone="no" ?>
<SnowReport>
	<_LastUpdated>2026-07-18T07:08:08</_LastUpdated>
	<TwentyFourHourSnowfall>0</TwentyFourHourSnowfall>
	<CurrentSnowdepth>41</CurrentSnowdepth>
	<AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>107</AvSnowdepthinSnowmakingAreas>
	<AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>17/07/2026</AvSnowdepthLastMeasuredOn>
	<DateofLastSnowfall>14/07/2026</DateofLastSnowfall>
	<SevenDaySnowfall>26</SevenDaySnowfall>
	<SeasonToDateSnowfall>96</SeasonToDateSnowfall>
	<Snowmaking>On Standby</Snowmaking>
	<WeatherConditionsasat6AM></WeatherConditionsasat6AM>
	<Temperature>6.4</Temperature>
	<OvernightMin>4.7</OvernightMin>
	<ForecastMax>9</ForecastMax>
	<Wind>Calm 0-10 km/h</Wind>
	<WindDirection>SSE</WindDirection>
	<Precipitation>None</Precipitation>
	<ExpectedVisibility>Excellent</ExpectedVisibility>
	<ExpectedCloudCover>Sunny</ExpectedCloudCover>
	<Forecast>Sunny. Light winds.</Forecast>
	<ResortReportHeadline>Slap on the sunscreen!</ResortReportHeadline>
	<ResortReport><p>It'll be another glorious sunny and calm day on the mountain.&nbsp; The sky is clear, the current temp is 6.4 degrees, there is no wind, and we are heading for a very warm top temp of 9 degrees.&nbsp; Although it's been warm overnight, the snow has had a low humidity freeze, and it will be firm first thing before the sunny aspects start to soften for fun spring-like conditions.</p><p>The Summit will open at 7.30am this morning, followed by all other scheduled lifts at 8.30am. Night Skiing at the Big D is on until 8.30pm tonight.&nbsp;</p><p>Heavenly Valley Lift is closed and the area is not recommended. The cover is marginal and the surface will remain icy all day on the shaded aspects.&nbsp; The conditions are only suitable for advanced and expert skiers and riders, so think about your group's ability before traversing across from the Summit or Road Runner.&nbsp; The Extreme Area is closed due to an extreme ice hazard.&nbsp; Do not enter!</p><p>Slap on the screen and have a beaut day on the hill.&nbsp;</p><p>Cheers Bill</p><p><br></p></ResortReport>
	<HideReportEDMWeb>False</HideReportEDMWeb>
	<LiftsOpen></LiftsOpen>
	<LiftsStandby></LiftsStandby>
	<LiftsClosed></LiftsClosed>
	<RunsOpen></RunsOpen>
	<RunsGroomed></RunsGroomed>
	<RunsSnowmaking>5</RunsSnowmaking>
	<TerrainParks></TerrainParks>
	<Backcountry>The cover is marginal and there is a significant slide hazard in the backcountry.   The sunniest of aspects may soften for spring-like turns as the day warms but shaded and south facing slopes will likely stay very slick and dangerous for the day.  Take care. </Backcountry>
	<Avalanche></Avalanche>
	<HeliLink>Closed</HeliLink>
</SnowReport>
